title: Cancel a Manual Bag Out for Exchange Tasks Not in Process
summary: Use the cancel bag out function during a manual bag out to cancel exchange tasks that are not already in process. Any exchange already in process must be allowed to finish.
when_to_use: Use during a manual bag out when exchange tasks need to be canceled before they begin processing.
not_for: Do not use this procedure with the expectation that an exchange already in process will be canceled.
Do not use this procedure as a rollback for in-process exchange work, because the source states that if an item is in an exchange, it has to finish.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the manual bag out controls, use the cancel bag out option, and verify that only exchange tasks not already in process are canceled.

safety_notes: The source describes this as a control behavior for manual bag out and does not provide additional safety hazards or lockout requirements.
Do not assume in-process exchanges can be interrupted; the source states they must finish.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to manual bag out controls
Visibility to exchange task state or in-process status

access_required: Access to the manual bag out controls
system_preconditions: A manual bag out is in effect or being managed
Exchange task state or in-process status is visible to the operator

visual_references.description: Training slide showing overall system bag out control behavior, including the cancel bag out capability for manual bag out.

steps.title: Access the manual bag out controls
steps.purpose: Reach the control area where the cancel bag out option is available during manual bag out.
steps.instruction: Open or access the manual bag out controls used for bag out management.
steps.expected_result: The manual bag out controls are visible and available for operator use.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can view the bag out control area associated with manual bag out.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot access or identify the manual bag out controls from the available interface.

actions.label: Open manual bag out controls
actions.exact_action: Access the manual bag out controls.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall system bag out control slide showing bag out options and the cancel bag out capability for manual bag out.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the manual bag out controls cannot be accessed or identified from the available interface.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In the event of a manual bag out Fortna offers a cancel bag out that will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.

steps.title: Identify the cancel bag out option
steps.purpose: Locate the specific control used to cancel eligible exchange tasks during manual bag out.
steps.instruction: Within the manual bag out controls, identify the cancel bag out option provided for manual bag out.
steps.expected_result: The cancel bag out option is identified and ready to be used.
steps.healthy_condition: A cancel bag out option is present within the manual bag out controls.
steps.failure_condition: The cancel bag out option cannot be found or is not available where expected.

actions.label: Locate cancel bag out
actions.exact_action: Identify the cancel bag out option provided for manual bag out.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Bag out control content describing that manual bag out includes a cancel bag out option.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the cancel bag out option is not visible or cannot be confirmed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In the event of a manual bag out Fortna offers a cancel bag out that will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.

steps.title: Activate cancel bag out
steps.purpose: Cancel exchange tasks that have not already started processing.
steps.instruction: Activate cancel bag out to cancel exchange tasks that are not already in process.
steps.expected_result: Exchange tasks not already in process are canceled.
steps.healthy_condition: Only exchange tasks that are not in process are affected by the cancel bag out action.
steps.failure_condition: Tasks outside the documented scope are affected, or eligible not-in-process exchange tasks are not canceled.

actions.label: Run cancel bag out
actions.exact_action: Activate cancel bag out to cancel exchange tasks that are not already in process.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The bag out control reference describing cancel bag out behavior for exchange tasks not in process.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the system behavior does not match the documented behavior for cancel bag out.
Escalate if the system appears to cancel in-process exchange work contrary to the documented behavior.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In the event of a manual bag out Fortna offers a cancel bag out that will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.

steps.title: Allow in-process exchanges to finish
steps.purpose: Apply the documented limitation that exchanges already in process cannot be canceled.
steps.instruction: Check whether any exchange task is already in process and allow that exchange to finish, because the source states it cannot be canceled once in exchange.
steps.expected_result: Any exchange already in process continues until finished.
steps.healthy_condition: In-process exchanges are allowed to complete and are not interrupted by the cancel bag out action.
steps.failure_condition: An in-process exchange is expected to stop, or appears to be canceled contrary to the documented behavior.

actions.label: Check exchange state
actions.exact_action: Check whether any exchange task is already in process.

actions.label: Allow in-process exchange to complete
actions.exact_action: Allow that exchange to finish because the source states it cannot be canceled once in exchange.

stop_or_escalate_if: Do not expect an exchange already in process to be canceled; the source states it must finish.
Escalate if the system appears to cancel in-process exchange work contrary to the documented behavior.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If it's in an exchange, it has to finish.

steps.title: Verify cancellation outcome
steps.purpose: Confirm that the cancel bag out action affected only the intended exchange tasks.
steps.instruction: Verify that only exchange tasks not in process are canceled.
steps.expected_result: Exchange tasks not already in process are canceled, while any exchange already in process continues until finished.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed result matches the documented behavior: not-in-process exchange tasks are canceled and in-process exchanges finish.
steps.failure_condition: In-process exchanges are canceled, or not-in-process exchange tasks remain active without explanation.

actions.label: Confirm task outcomes
actions.exact_action: Verify that only exchange tasks not in process are canceled.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the system appears to cancel in-process exchange work contrary to the documented behavior.
Escalate if the observed cancellation outcome does not match the documented behavior.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In the event of a manual bag out Fortna offers a cancel bag out that will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.
If it's in an exchange, it has to finish.

success_criteria: Exchange tasks not already in process are canceled.
Any exchange already in process continues until finished.
Observed system behavior matches the documented cancel bag out behavior.
healthy_conditions: The cancel bag out option is available during manual bag out.
Not-in-process exchange tasks are canceled after the action is activated.
In-process exchanges are allowed to finish.
failure_conditions: The cancel bag out option cannot be accessed or identified.
The system appears to cancel exchange work that is already in process.
The source does not describe additional rollback or confirmation behavior after cancellation.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the system appears to cancel in-process exchange work contrary to the documented behavior.
Escalate if the cancel bag out option is unavailable when manual bag out is being managed.
Escalate if observed results do not match the documented rule that only exchange tasks not in process are canceled.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: In the event of a manual bag out Fortna offers a cancel bag out that will cancel any exchange task that is not in process.
If it's in an exchange, it has to finish.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact button labels for the cancel bag out control.
The source does not provide exact task-state indicators for determining whether an exchange is in process.
The source does not describe additional rollback behavior after cancellation.
The source does not describe confirmation dialogs, prompts, or post-action notifications.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this procedure.
